title: Curacao vs. Ivory Coast prediction, odds: World Cup picks, best bets for Group E
original_text:
Curacao vs. Ivory Coast prediction, odds: World Cup picks, best bets for Group E
Ivory Coast XI vs Curacao: Starting lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today
Thu, June 25, 2026 at 2:43 PM EDT
Ivory Coast face Curacao tonight, looking to qualify for the World Cup knockout stage for the first time in their history.
The Elephants beat Ecuador in their opener thanks to a last-gasp Amad Diallo goal, but suffered a late heartbreak of their own after Denis Undav secured a 2-1 for Germany in the 94th-minute.
CLICK HERE TO FOLLOW CURACAO VS IVORY COAST LIVE!
Coach Emerse Fae - who was in charge for their Africa Cup of Nations title win in 2023 - started off the tournament with a 4-4-2 formation, with Manchester United winger Amad surprisingly on the bench.
In the Germany game, Fae started Amad and switched to a 4-1-4-1 formation, making five changes: he dropped right-back Guela Doue (brother of France and Paris Saint-Germain star Desire), central midfielder Seko Fofana and forwards Nicolas Pepe, Elye Wahi, and Bazoumana Toure.
Odilon Kossounou came in at centre back, while Ibrahima Sangare acted as a sole defensive screen at the base of midfield. Inao Oulai partnered Franck Kessie, while Ange-Yoan Bonny was the lone striker. Diallo replaced Pepe on the right-wing.
Fae's changes almost worked as they led Germany until the 68th minute through Kessie's first-half goal, before Undav's late heroics.
Franck Kessie opened the scoring for Ivory Coast against Germany (Getty)
There will be at least one enforced change as Wilfried Singo is set for a period of rest after avoiding a serious injury in the loss to Germany. He makes the bench but Doue replaces him at right back.
Amad keeps his place, while Yan Diomande has played on both wings in the tournament and does the same. Nicolas Pepe also gets the nod.
Yahia Fofana made several good saves against Germany, and could do little about either of Undav's finishes, so remains between the sticks.
Ivory Coast XI: Fofana, O. Diomande, Kossounou, Operi, Doue, Kessie, Sangare, Bonny, Y. Diomande, Diallo, Pepe
Subs: Kone, Lafont, Konan, Singo, Agbadou, Ndicka, Seri, S. Fofana, Guiagon, Oulai, Adingra, Wahi, Diakite, Guessand, Toure
Time and date: 9pm BST on Thursday, June 25, 2026
How to watch: ITV
Venue: Philadelphia Stadium
Mike Turay
Thu, June 25, 2026 at 12:00 PM EDT

An image collage containing 2 images, Image 1 shows Yan Diomande of Ivory Coast in action during the FIFA World Cup 2026 group stage match against Germany, Image 2 shows Curacao's goalkeeper Eloy Room in his uniform during the FIFA World Cup 2026 match against Ecuador
Curacao and the Ivory Coast conclude Group E play in the World Cup on Thursday at Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia.
The Elephants are firmly in second place in the group, earning 3 points with a 1-0 win over Ecuador thanks to the clutch goal of Amad Diallo, but could not withstand a pair of Deniz Undav goals and a German rally, which led to a brutal 2-1 loss in their second contest.
It's been a moral victory tour for the Blue Wave amid their World Cup debut; they enter as one of the better-known last-place teams remaining in the bracket.
After a humbling 7-1 defeat to the Germans in the opener, goalkeeper Eloy Room's record-setting 15 saves against Ecuador settled them to a scoreless draw.
Côte d'Ivoire is a hefty -625 favorite on the three-way moneyline, with Curacao's odds to win set at 14/1 and a draw at +700 on BetMGM Sportsbook.
Curacao vs Ivory Coast prediction, best bet
The Elephants sat just 22 minutes shy of a massive upset against Germany, powered by a masterclass performance from Yan Diomande and his relentless effort.
Though he may lack the mainstream hype surrounding a Lamine Yamal or Desire Doue, the 19-year-old's rapid rise has established him as a premier breakout star in this tournament.
This spells trouble for a vulnerable Curacao defense that conceded seven goals to Germany.
Curacao's Eloy Room has become a household name during the World Cup. IMAGN IMAGES via Reuters
While Curacao's goalkeeper has been a strong presence, Ecuador's recent 27-shot effort suggests the Ivory Coast's attack could have a big opportunity.
Emerse Fae's dynamic offense, led by Diomande, Diallo, and Ange-Yoan Bonny, should take advantage of some promising transition moments.
Get the lowdown on the Best USA Sports Betting Sites and Apps
This team is aiming to reach the Group Stage knockout round for the first time, a milestone that would please former stars Didier Drogba and Yaya Toure.
I believe Côte d'Ivoire will surpass Curacao and secure a trip to the Round of 32 in convincing fashion.
The Play: Ivory Coast over 2.5 goals (-120, BetMGM) | Yan Diomande anytime goalscorer (+130, bet365)
